title: Chief Rabbi says attacks 'gathering momentum' after another synagogue targeted
summary_cn: 伦敦犹太社区近期发生多起纵火袭击事件，首席拉比警告针对犹太人的攻击正在加剧。反恐警察正在调查这些事件是否与可能和伊朗有关的组织有关联，肇事者将面临国家安全法的严厉起诉。
paragraphs_en: Attacks on the Jewish community are "gathering momentum", the Chief Rabbi says, after another arson attack was reported at a north-west London synagogue.
The warning comes after Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow was attacked overnight, the latest in a series of arson attacks on Jewish properties this month.

Counter-terrorism police are investigating whether they are linked to a group with possible ties to Iran, with officers warning those involved will face serious prosecution under national security laws.
Chief Rabbi Sir Ephraim Mirvis described the latest attack as "cowardly" while Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer said he was appalled and that "visible policing" would be increased.

At a press briefing outside Kenton United Synagogue on Sunday, deputy assistant commissioner Vicki Evans said the incidents were similar in nature and had been claimed online by a group called Ashab al‑Yamin, which had also claimed attacks on Jewish targets across Europe.
"We are aware of public reporting that suggests this group may have links to Iran - as you would expect we will continue to explore that question as our investigation evolves," she said.
"I've spoken previously about the Iranian regime's use of criminal proxies, and we're considering whether this tactic is being used here in London.
"This is recruiting violence as a service, and the people who conduct that violence often have little or no allegiance to the cause and are taking quick cash for their crimes."
Evans added anyone who was identified could be prosecuted under the National Security Act, which carried "significant sentences and lifetime restrictions".

The Met said counter-terrorism officers were investigating the latest attack as well as one at Finchley Reform Synagogue, another at a building used by a Jewish charity in Hendon, and ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity set alight in Golders Green last month.
An arson attack at a Persian language media organisation is also being investigated.
At Kenton synagogue, the Met said officers on "deterrence and reassurance patrols" noticed damage to a window overnight.
"On further inspection they saw smoke inside a room and evidence that a bottle with some sort of accelerant had been thrown through the window," the force said.
"The London Fire Brigade was called and firefighters searched the building to confirm that there was no further fire risk."
No-one was injured and minor smoke damage was reported.
Yehuda Black, the synagogue's rabbi, said the item was thrown into the medical room.
"I was very upset to hear about what happened. Our synagogue is a beautiful synagogue," he said.
"It's so unfortunate that it has been damaged in such a way."
The synagogue was closed following the attack but there were hopes it would reopen in a few days.
Sir Ephraim wrote on social media: "Last night yet another synagogue, this time in Kenton, was targeted in a cowardly arson attack."
He added: "A sustained campaign of violence and intimidation against the Jewish community of the UK is gathering momentum.
"This sustained attack on our community's ability to worship and live in safety is an attack on the values that bind us all together.
"Thank God, no lives have been lost, but we cannot, and must not, wait for that to change before we understand just how dangerous this moment is for all of our society."
On social media, Sir Keir wrote: "This is abhorrent and it will not be tolerated. Attacks on our Jewish community are attacks on Britain.
"We are increasing visible policing and those responsible will be found and brought to justice. We will not rest in the pursuit of perpetrators."
Sir Sadiq Khan said "history shows that we will never back down against terrorists".

In a statement, the mayor of London added: "There can never be any justification for attacking our Jewish friends, neighbours and colleagues. Those responsible are seeking to promote fear, hatred and division.
"They can't stand what our city represents - an open, diverse, pluralistic society that embraces and celebrates our differences."
The Community Security Trust (CST), an organisation which provides security for Jewish people, said thanked the police and London Fire Brigade for responding quickly.
"We are supporting the affected location and are working closely with the police as they investigate and seek to identify those responsible," a spokesperson added.
The Campaign Against Antisemitism said this was now "terrifyingly becoming a spate of daily arson attacks on the Jewish community".
United Synagogue, an organisation that Kenton synagogue is a part of, said: "A synagogue is not just a building, it is a place of worship, community and - theoretically - safety.
"To target it in this way is a deeply disturbing act that strikes at the heart of Jewish life."
The organisation's president, Saul Taylor, added: "The prime minister should declare publicly what the Jewish community has known for some time, this is an epidemic of anti-Jewish hate."

On Saturday evening the Met Police said it was increasing patrols across north‑west London, with more officers, stop and search powers, and armed and counter‑terrorism units deployed to protect communities and deter further incidents.
Listen to the best of BBC Radio London on Sounds and follow BBC London on Facebook , X and Instagram . Send your story ideas to hello.bbclondon@bbc.co.uk
paragraphs_cn: 首席拉比表示，在伦敦西北部一座犹太教堂再次发生纵火袭击后，针对犹太社区的袭击正在“加速升级”。
这一警告是在哈罗区肯顿联合犹太教堂（Kenton United Synagogue）夜间遭袭后发出的，这是本月一系列针对犹太场所纵火袭击中的最新一起。
反恐警察正在调查这些袭击是否与一个可能与伊朗有联系的团体有关，警方警告称涉案人员将根据国家安全法面临严厉起诉。
首席拉比以法莲·米尔维斯爵士（Sir Ephraim Mirvis）称最新袭击“懦弱”，而首相基尔·斯塔默爵士（Sir Keir Starmer）表示感到震惊，并称将增加“显性警力部署”。
周日，在肯顿联合犹太教堂外的新闻发布会上，副助理专员维姬·埃文斯（Vicki Evans）表示，这些事件性质相似，一个名为“阿什布·亚敏”（Ashab al‑Yamin）的团体已在网上宣称负责，该团体还声称对欧洲各地的犹太目标发动了袭击。
她说：“我们注意到公开报道称该团体可能与伊朗有联系——正如你们所料，随着调查的深入，我们将继续探讨这个问题。”
“我之前曾谈到伊朗政权利用犯罪代理人的做法，我们正在考虑这种策略是否在伦敦被使用。”
“这是在招募暴力服务，实施这种暴力的人往往对所谓的事业几乎没有忠诚度，只是为了快速赚钱而犯罪。”
埃文斯补充说，任何被识别的人员都可能根据《国家安全法》被起诉，该法规定了“重刑和终身限制”。
伦敦警察厅表示，反恐官员正在调查最新袭击，以及芬奇利改革派犹太教堂（Finchley Reform Synagogue）的袭击、亨登一座犹太慈善机构使用的建筑遭袭，以及上个月戈尔德斯格林（Golders Green）属于一家犹太慈善机构的救护车被纵火的事件。
一起针对波斯语媒体机构的纵火袭击也在调查中。
关于肯顿犹太教堂，伦敦警察厅表示，执行“威慑与安抚巡逻”的警员夜间发现一扇窗户受损。
警方称：“进一步检查时，他们看到房间内有烟雾，并有证据表明一个装有某种助燃剂的瓶子被扔进了窗户。”
“伦敦消防队被呼叫，消防员搜查了建筑以确认没有进一步的火灾风险。”
无人受伤，仅报告有轻微烟熏损坏。
犹太教堂的拉比耶胡达·布莱克（Yehuda Black）表示，物品被扔进了医疗室。
他说：“听到发生的事情我非常难过。我们的犹太教堂是一座美丽的犹太教堂。”
“以这种方式受损真是太不幸了。”
袭击后犹太教堂已关闭，但有望在几天内重新开放。
以法莲爵士在社交媒体上写道：“昨晚又一座犹太教堂，这次是肯顿的，成为懦弱纵火袭击的目标。”
他补充说：“针对英国犹太社区的持续暴力和恐吓运动正在加速升级。”
“这种对我们社区安全礼拜和生活能力的持续攻击，是对将我们所有人团结在一起的价值观的攻击。”
“感谢上帝，尚无人员伤亡，但我们不能也不应等到情况改变才意识到这一刻对我们整个社会有多么危险。”
在社交媒体上，基尔爵士写道：“这是可憎的，不会被容忍。对我们犹太社区的袭击就是对英国的袭击。”
“我们正在增加显性警力部署，责任人将被找到并绳之以法。我们不会停止追捕肇事者。”
萨迪克·汗爵士（Sir Sadiq Khan）表示“历史表明我们永远不会向恐怖分子低头”。
伦敦市长在一份声明中补充说：“攻击我们的犹太朋友、邻居和同事永远没有任何正当理由。责任人试图宣扬恐惧、仇恨和分裂。”
“他们无法容忍我们城市所代表的——一个开放、多元、包容差异并为之庆祝的多元社会。”
为犹太人提供安全的组织社区安全信托（Community Security Trust，CST）感谢警方和伦敦消防队的快速响应。
一位发言人补充说：“我们正在支持受影响的地点，并与警方密切合作进行调查，以确定责任人。”
反反犹主义运动（Campaign Against Antisemitism）表示，这现在“可怕地成为针对犹太社区的每日纵火袭击浪潮”。
肯顿犹太教堂所属的组织联合犹太教堂（United Synagogue）表示：“犹太教堂不仅仅是一座建筑，它是礼拜、社区和——理论上——安全的场所。”
“以这种方式攻击它，是一种深深令人不安的行为，直击犹太生活的核心。”
该组织主席索尔·泰勒（Saul Taylor）补充说：“首相应公开宣布犹太社区已知晓一段时间的事实，这是一场反犹太仇恨的流行病。”
周六晚间，伦敦警察厅表示正在增加伦敦西北部的巡逻，部署更多警员、使用拦截搜查权，并派出武装和反恐单位以保护社区并威慑进一步事件。

keywords.definition_en: The force or speed of movement that makes something continue or grow.
keywords.context_sentence: A sustained campaign of violence and intimidation against the Jewish community of the UK is gathering momentum.
keywords.context_translation: 针对英国犹太社区的持续暴力和恐吓活动正在积聚势头。

keywords.definition_en: The criminal act of deliberately setting fire to property.
keywords.context_sentence: The warning comes after Kenton United Synagogue in Harrow was attacked overnight, the latest in a series of arson attacks on Jewish properties this month.
keywords.context_translation: 此前，哈罗的肯顿联合犹太教堂在一夜之间遭到袭击，这是本月一系列针对犹太财产的纵火袭击中的最新一起。

keywords.definition_en: The act or process of bringing a criminal case against someone in court.
keywords.context_sentence: Counter-terrorism police are investigating whether they are linked to a group with possible ties to Iran, with officers warning those involved will face serious prosecution under national security laws.
keywords.context_translation: 反恐警察正在调查这些事件是否与一个可能与伊朗有联系的组织有关，警官警告称，涉案人员将根据国家安全法面临严厉起诉。

keywords.definition_en: A substance used to speed up the spread of fire.
keywords.context_sentence: "On further inspection they saw smoke inside a room and evidence that a bottle with some sort of accelerant had been thrown through the window," the force said.
keywords.context_translation: 警方表示：“进一步检查后，他们发现房间内有烟雾，并有证据表明一个装有某种助燃剂的瓶子被扔进了窗户。”

keywords.definition_en: The action of discouraging an action or event through instilling doubt or fear of consequences.
keywords.context_sentence: At Kenton synagogue, the Met said officers on "deterrence and reassurance patrols" noticed damage to a window overnight.
keywords.context_translation: 在肯顿犹太教堂，伦敦警察厅表示，执行“威慑和安抚巡逻”的警官注意到窗户在一夜之间受损。

keywords.context_sentence: On social media, Sir Keir wrote: "This is abhorrent and it will not be tolerated. Attacks on our Jewish community are attacks on Britain."
keywords.context_translation: 基尔爵士在社交媒体上写道：“这是令人憎恶的，不会被容忍。对我们犹太社区的袭击就是对英国的袭击。”

keywords.definition_en: People who carry out a harmful, illegal, or immoral act.
keywords.context_sentence: We will not rest in the pursuit of perpetrators.
keywords.context_translation: 我们将不遗余力地追捕犯罪者。

keywords.definition_en: Relating to or advocating a system in which multiple groups coexist and maintain their distinct identities.
keywords.context_sentence: They can't stand what our city represents - an open, diverse, pluralistic society that embraces and celebrates our differences.
keywords.context_translation: 他们无法忍受我们的城市所代表的——一个开放、多样、多元的社会，它接纳并庆祝我们的差异。
